title: Subtracting From a Number With Zeros (400 − 167)

steps.instruction: Find 400 − 167. Look at the ones and tens digits of 400.
steps.work: 400 has 0 ones and 0 tens. We cannot subtract 7 from 0 or 6 from 0.
steps.explanation: When digits are 0, we must regroup from a higher place.

steps.instruction: Borrow from the hundreds and pass it through the tens to the ones.
steps.work: 400 → 3 hundreds, 10 tens, 0 ones → 3 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones.
steps.explanation: Take 1 hundred (making 3 hundreds), turn it into 10 tens. Then take 1 of those tens (making 9 tens) and turn it into 10 ones.
After regrouping, each subtraction is straightforward.
The key trick: regroup the hundred into 9 tens and 10 ones in one step.

steps.work: 1000 has three zeros. We need to regroup from the thousands place.
steps.explanation: Regrouping passes through hundreds, tens, and ones — all zeros.

steps.instruction: Turn 1000 into hundreds, tens, and ones.
steps.explanation: Borrow 1 thousand → 10 hundreds. Borrow 1 hundred → 10 tens. Borrow 1 ten → 10 ones. Left with 9 hundreds, 9 tens, 10 ones.
Each digit on top is now larger than the digit below.
Tip: for 1000 − anything, the pattern is always 9-9-10 after regrouping.
